foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Normal wound healing depends on intact hemostatic and inflammatory cascades; understanding tissue oxygenation and perfusion physiology is essential

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Protein-calorie malnutrition, vitamin C and zinc deficiency directly impair collagen synthesis and wound tensile strength

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Burn wound healing involves unique challenges including contraction, hypertrophic scarring, and the need for grafting in deep burns

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Aseptic wound management and sterile dressing technique are fundamental to promoting primary wound healing

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Wound infection is the most common cause of impaired healing and dehiscence; SSI prevention is integral to wound management

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Chronic non-healing wounds (Marjolin's ulcer) can undergo malignant transformation; wound biology underpins excision and reconstruction planning

objectives: SU5.1: Describe normal wound healing and factors affecting healing.
SU5.2: Elicit, document and present a history in a patient presenting with C
SU5.3: Differentiate the various types of wounds, plan and observe
SU5.4: Discuss medico legal aspects of wounds
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Wound Healing across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
